meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 6)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Identify common and proper nouns using context clues and real-life examples.

questions.question: Which word is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "Taipei 101" is a proper noun — it's the specific name of a building.

questions.question: "Tuesday" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! Tuesday is a day of the week — a proper noun that must be capitalized.

questions.question: What type of noun is "newspaper"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: newspaper is a common noun — it refers to any newspaper.

questions.question: Which word is a common noun?
questions.explanation: "newspaper" is a common noun — it refers to any newspaper.

questions.question: "Costco" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! Costco is a proper noun — it's the specific name of a store.

questions.question: What type of noun is "November"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: November is a proper noun — months are specific names and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: newspaper/singer = general words (common). Taipei 101/Costco/November = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "The library is very quiet," the word "library" should be capitalized.
questions.explanation: "library" is a common noun — it stays lowercase unless it's a specific library name.

questions.question: Which sentence has correct capitalization?
options: I like to play uno with my friends.
I like to play Uno with my friends.
I Like to Play Uno With My Friends.
I like to play UNO with my friends.
questions.explanation: "Uno" is a proper noun (specific game name). Only proper nouns need capitals.

questions.question: What type of noun is "hotel"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: It's a general type of building, not a specific name
questions.explanation: hotel is a common noun — it refers to any hotel. A specific hotel name (like Marriott) would be proper.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "I like to go to Costco." Costco is a proper noun.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: hotel/city = general words (common). Ms. Betty/Taipei 101/Monday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: "Father's Day" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! Father's Day is a specific holiday name — a proper noun that must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence is written correctly?
options: We go to school on tuesday.
We go to school on Tuesday.
We Go To School On Tuesday.
We go to School on Tuesday.
questions.explanation: "Tuesday" (day of the week = proper noun) must be capitalized. "school" is common.

questions.question: Fix this sentence: 'We buy snacks at familymart.' Write the store name correctly.
questions.hint: The store name is a proper noun
questions.explanation: FamilyMart is a proper noun (specific store name) — it must be capitalized.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "We buy snacks at FamilyMart." FamilyMart is a proper noun.

questions.question: Which sentence has ALL nouns correctly capitalized?
questions.explanation: "bus" and "big" are common words (not proper nouns) — they stay lowercase. The sentence is correct.

questions.question: In the sentence "My pencil is yellow," the noun "pencil" should be capitalized.
questions.explanation: "pencil" is a common noun — it does NOT need a capital letter.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: band/song = general words (common). John/FamilyMart/Friday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: Fix the sentence: 'a cow is cute.' What type of noun is 'cow'? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: cow is a common noun — it refers to any cow. Only the first word of the sentence needs a capital.
